wioHero.headline:
We Automate Agency Operations
So Your Team Doesn't Have To
wioHero.subheadline: Meticulosity.io is a managed agency operations automation service. We plug into the tools you already use, deploy workflow automations that replace repetitive ops work, and run everything on an ongoing basis. Your team never builds or maintains a workflow. That's our job.

threeThings.intro: There are three things that make Meticulosity.io different from every other automation tool, consultant, or platform you've looked at. Get these three things and you'll understand why agencies that try it don't go back.

cards.title: It's Not Software
cards.subtitle: There's no platform to log into.
cards.body: Meticulosity.io is not a tool you buy. There are no dashboards to learn, no drag-and-drop builders, no workflow templates to figure out. You don't need a technical team member to make it work. We handle everything behind the scenes. You experience the results.

cards.title: It's Not Consulting
cards.subtitle: We don't hand you a playbook and wish you luck.
cards.body: We don't teach you how to fish. We do the fishing for you, then serve it tableside. There's no training phase where your team tries to learn a new system. No "here's a recommended workflow" document that collects dust. We deploy working automations and operate them. Permanently.

cards.title: It's a Managed Service
cards.subtitle: We deploy, run, and maintain automations across your existing tools.
cards.body: Think of it like outsourcing your bookkeeping or IT. Except instead of accounting or helpdesk, we handle the operational busywork that's quietly eating 15 to 20 percent of your margins: onboarding, burn reports, invoicing, task routing, and dozens more. We monitor everything. When something changes, we handle it.

wioHowItWorks.headline: Three Steps. Then It Just Runs.
wioHowItWorks.intro: Every engagement follows the same proven path, refined across 60+ agency clients. The process is fast, non-disruptive, and designed to deliver measurable results within weeks, not months.

steps.title: We Connect Your Data
steps.body: Before a single automation runs, we connect your project management tool, accounting system, CRM, and communications into one shared data layer: a single connected view of your operations.
steps.details: Think of it as a master roster that always knows which clients are active and what they purchased, who's working on what and how many hours they've logged, which tasks trace back to which clients and budgets, and what's been invoiced versus what hasn't.
steps.extra: You never see or interact with the data layer directly. It runs in the background, stays in sync, and gives every automation a reliable foundation to work from.
steps.callout: Without a data layer, each automation would need its own fragile way of looking things up. With it, every automation draws from the same single source of truth.

steps.title: We Deploy Automations
steps.body: From our library of 50+ automations running live across multiple agencies, we activate the ones that match your operations. Burn reports. Client onboarding. Time tracking sync. Budget alerts. AI-powered task triage. And more.
steps.details: Every automation has a pre-measured "hours saved" value based on how long that work takes a person to do manually. Your monthly investment is based on the total hours saved, not guesswork.
steps.extra: We don't deploy everything at once. We start with the highest-impact automations, prove they work, and expand from there.

steps.title: We Run Everything
steps.body: This is where managed service means something. We monitor, maintain, and optimize every automation on an ongoing basis. When something breaks, we fix it. When your operations change, we adapt. When a better approach exists, we upgrade.
steps.details: Your team never touches a workflow. Nobody on your staff needs to understand how the automations work, troubleshoot errors, or remember to check that things are running. That's entirely on us.

wioHowItWorks.bottomCallout: At Meticulosity, we run this exact system across 60+ agency clients. It saves over 230 hours of manual work every month: the equivalent of 1.5 full-time hires, running around the clock.

dataLayerDeep.headline: Before We Automate Anything, We Connect Your Data
dataLayerDeep.subheadline: Every automation depends on one thing: a single source of truth.
dataLayerDeep.problemIntro: Right now, your agency's data lives in 8 to 12 different tools. Your PM tool knows who's working on what. Your accounting system knows who paid. Your CRM knows deal stages. But no single system can answer the question that actually matters:
dataLayerDeep.pullQuote: How many hours has this client used out of the hours they purchased?
dataLayerDeep.problemBody: Getting that answer today means exporting data, reconciling spreadsheets, and hoping nothing was missed.
dataLayerDeep.crisisStory: We learned this the hard way. An export bug in our project management tool left over 100 hours of legitimate billable work unreported, costing nearly $20,000 in unrecoverable revenue. Every agency knows the unwritten rule: you can't go back to a client weeks later and ask them to pay for time you forgot to invoice. That money was gone.
dataLayerDeep.crisisConclusion: That crisis is why the data layer exists.
dataLayerDeep.whatItDoes: The data layer connects your tools into one shared view of your operations. Every time entry traces back to a task, which traces to a client, which traces to purchased hours. That chain is what makes burn reports, budget alerts, and every other automation possible.

features.title: Client and Revenue Data
features.description: Which clients are active, what they purchased, what's been invoiced, and what hasn't. Your accounting system and CRM feed into a single view that always reflects reality.

features.title: Task and Project Data
features.description: Every task in your PM tool syncs to the data layer with its client, assignee, status, priority, and timestamps. This is how the system knows what work is happening and who it belongs to.

features.title: Time Entry Data
features.description: The heartbeat of the entire system. Every time entry records who logged it, which task it belongs to, and how long it took. This is the raw material that powers burn reports, utilization tracking, and capacity planning.

features.title: Service Catalog
features.description: A standardized map of everything your agency delivers. Each service has a time estimate and links to your processes. When a task is created, it maps to your catalog, so the system knows what type of work it is and how to categorize it.

dataLayerDeep.howItConnects: A time entry is logged in your PM tool. It syncs to the data layer. It's linked to a task, which traces back to a client, who has a defined number of purchased hours. The system can now calculate utilization in real time: "Client X has used 47.3 hours out of their 60-hour block this month." No exports. No spreadsheets. No reconciliation.
dataLayerDeep.howItConnectsIntro: The power isn't in any single piece. It's in how they relate to each other.
dataLayerDeep.builtForAi: AI is only as reliable as the data it draws from. Our AI-powered automations don't guess. They draw from clean, connected, structured data that we build and maintain. That's why they work reliably at scale, and that's why "just plug in ChatGPT" doesn't produce the same results.
dataLayerDeep.atScale: At Meticulosity, our data layer connects 60+ clients across every tool in their stack. It's the reason we can run so many automations reliably. Without it, each automation would need its own fragile way of looking things up. With it, every automation draws from the same single source of truth.
dataLayerDeep.callout: You don't build it. You don't maintain it. You don't even log into it. We handle all of that as part of the service.

featuredAutomations.headline: 50+ Live Automations. Here's What They Replace.
featuredAutomations.intro: Every automation in our library was built because something broke, was forgotten, took too long, or cost too much when done manually. They run in production across multiple agencies today. Here are the ones that make the biggest immediate difference.

automations.name: Client Time Tracking Burn Reports
automations.body: Automatically generates personalized burn reports for every active client, archives them in Google Drive, and delivers them by email. Weekly summaries every Friday. Monthly reports at end-of-month. Nobody touches a spreadsheet.
automations.pain: Someone on your team spends every Friday afternoon grinding through query, export, format, email, repeat. When that person is out sick, the reports don't go out. Clients notice, and they start wondering what else is being missed.
automations.scale: At Meticulosity, we spent about 10 to 15 minutes per client per week preparing burn reports, then again at month-end. With 60 clients, we're saving about 50 hours per month automating the entire process.

automations.name: AI Call Transcript Processor
automations.body: After every client call, the recording is transcribed and matched to the right client. AI cross-references the transcript against active tasks, extracts action items and deadlines, and writes a concise summary that posts to Slack. Every task discussed on the call gets a comment added automatically in your PM tool with the relevant context.
automations.pain: After every client call, someone needs to write up notes, update the PM tool, and notify the right people. In practice, this rarely happens consistently. The account manager gets pulled into the next meeting, details fade, and action items slip through the cracks.
automations.scale: If each call takes 15 to 20 minutes to process manually and you average 3 calls per client per week, a 30-client agency burns 22+ hours per week on post-call administration. This automation runs those hours down to zero.

automations.name: New Client Onboarding
automations.body: Triggered when a proposal is signed. Automatically creates a PM workspace with your standard folder structure, a dedicated Slack channel, Google Drive folders, a QuickBooks customer record, and fires the onboarding email sequence. Every new client gets the same consistent setup, every time, in minutes instead of hours.
automations.pain: Onboarding is one of the first places agencies drop the ball at scale. When you're onboarding 4 to 6 clients per month, the manual setup work alone eats a full day. Miss a step and the project starts on the wrong foot.

automations.name: AI Work Order Triage
automations.body: When a client submits a work request, AI analyzes the submission, categorizes it by skill tier and complexity, identifies missing information, and suggests clarifying questions before work begins. The right team gets notified with context already attached.
automations.pain: Without triage, every incoming request lands on one person's plate. They read it, try to understand it, ask questions, wait for answers, then route it. Multiply that by 20 to 30 requests per week across 15+ clients and you've found your bottleneck.

catalogCta.body: These four are the automations that get the biggest reaction in discovery calls, but they represent a fraction of what's available. Our full library spans client lifecycle, project management, time and billing, team communication, AI-powered intelligence, and data synchronization.
catalogCta.label: View the Full Automation Catalog

aiSection.headline: AI Embedded in Workflows, Not Pasted from ChatGPT
aiSection.intro: Most agencies are already using AI. Someone opens ChatGPT, writes a prompt, reviews the output, copies it somewhere. The result: inconsistent quality, variance between team members, and adoption that depends entirely on who remembers to do it.
aiSection.howDifferent: Our AI runs inside operational workflows, not alongside them. Every AI-powered automation uses standardized prompts that produce consistent outcomes. No manual prompting. No copy-paste. No variance between team members. No dependence on one person remembering to use it.
aiSection.howDifferentFollowup: The value of AI in a service business isn't the AI itself. It's the automation layer that ensures AI is applied consistently, every time, without anyone having to remember.

examples.title: Task Triage
examples.description: AI reads incoming client requests and categorizes them by type, complexity, and skill tier. It identifies missing information and drafts clarifying questions before work begins.

examples.title: Call Transcript Processing
examples.description: AI matches transcripts to client task lists, identifies which tasks were discussed, extracts action items, and writes summaries that get posted directly to your PM tool.

examples.title: Work Summary Generation
examples.description: AI reviews completed work across your team and generates plain-english summaries for client-facing reports.

aiSection.callout: AI is only as reliable as the data it draws from. Our AI-powered automations don't guess. They draw from clean, connected data that we build and maintain. That's why they work reliably at scale, and why plugging AI into disconnected tools produces unreliable results.

numbers.headline: 230+ Hours Saved Per Month at Meticulosity. What Could It Do for Yours?
numbers.intro: These aren't projections. These are real numbers from running Meticulosity.io internally across 60+ agency clients.

rows.drivers: 60+ burn reports, time sync, budget enforcement
Task and time entry sync across systems

numbers.scaleCallout: Meticulosity is a boutique firm serving 60+ clients with a lean team. If our automations save 230+ hours per month at this scale, the impact for a larger agency would be significantly higher. The automations scale with your client count: every new client increases the hours saved without increasing the work required from your team.

toolsGrid.headline: We Work With the Tools You Already Use
toolsGrid.intro: Meticulosity.io is platform and tool agnostic. It doesn't matter if you run ClickUp or Monday.com, or whether you use Xero instead of QuickBooks Online. You don't rip down and rebuild. You use your tools.

whatItsNot.headline: Let's Be Clear About What This Isn't
whatItsNot.intro: We get these questions in almost every discovery call. Better to address them head-on.

cards.title: It's not a replacement for your PM tool.
cards.body: We sit on top of your existing tools. You keep ClickUp, Monday, Asana, or whatever you use. We don't ask you to migrate anything. Your team keeps working exactly where they work now.

cards.title: It's not a one-time implementation.
cards.body: We don't set things up and walk away. This is a managed service with ongoing monitoring, maintenance, and optimization. When things change, we adapt. When new automations make sense, we deploy them.

cards.title: It's not a DIY automation platform.
cards.body: Your team never has to build, configure, or maintain workflows. You don't need an "automation person" on staff. You don't need to learn a platform. We handle the entire technology layer.

wioOriginStory.headline: Built from Operational Necessity, Not Market Research
body: Meticulosity.io didn't start as a product idea. It started as a survival mechanism.

Meticulosity is a white-label HubSpot agency. We've been a HubSpot partner for over 11 years, hold Platinum status, and have completed over 5,800 projects. We operate as "The HubSpot Agency for Agencies," delivering services under partner agency brands.
Running 60+ agency clients across time zones and billing models forced a choice: automate or break. We chose to automate.

Over two years, we built and refined a library of 50+ automations, many AI-enhanced, that now save over 230 hours of manual work every month. That's the equivalent of 1.5 full-time hires, running around the clock at a fraction of the cost.

Every automation exists because something broke, was forgotten, took too long, or cost too much when done manually. This product was forged in the daily grind of running a real agency with real clients, real deadlines, and real consequences for mistakes.
Now we operate those same automations for you.

items.question: How long does it take to get up and running?
items.answer: Most agencies are live with their first automations within 2 to 4 weeks. We start with discovery (mapping your tools, identifying the highest-impact workflows), then build your data layer and deploy automations in phases. You don't wait months to see results.

items.question: Do we need to change how our team works?
items.answer: No. We plug into your existing tools and processes. Your team keeps working exactly where they work now. The only thing that changes is that repetitive ops work stops landing on someone's desk.

items.question: What if our tools aren't on your supported list?
items.answer: Our supported list covers the vast majority of agency tech stacks. If you use something unusual, we'll evaluate it during discovery. In most cases, if the tool has a modern integration layer, we can work with it.

items.question: How do we know it's actually saving time?
items.answer: Every automation has a pre-measured "hours saved" value based on actual manual timing. We report weekly and monthly on exactly which automations ran, how many times, and how many hours they saved. Complete transparency.

items.question: What happens if something breaks?
items.answer: We monitor everything. If an automation fails or behaves unexpectedly, our team is notified and handles the fix. You don't troubleshoot. You don't file a ticket. We own reliability.

items.question: Is there a long-term contract?
items.answer: We require a 4-month minimum commitment with no setup fees. After that, the service continues month-to-month. We earn your business through measurable results, not contractual lock-in.

items.question: How is this different from hiring an ops person?
items.answer: An ops hire in North America costs $80,000 to $120,000 per year. They get sick, take vacations, forget things, and can only work on one task at a time. Our automations run 24/7 with perfect consistency, starting at $500 per month. Most agencies see 6 to 10x ROI compared to the cost of equivalent headcount.

items.question: Can we start small and expand?
items.answer: That's exactly how we recommend it. Start with the automations that address your biggest pain points, prove the value, then expand as you see results. You only pay for hours saved, so pricing scales naturally.

wioFinalCta.headline: See What 230+ Hours of Recovered Capacity Looks Like
wioFinalCta.subheadline: Book a discovery call and we'll walk through your tools, your pain points, and the specific automations that would make the biggest difference for your agency. No pitch deck. Just a conversation about your operations.
wioFinalCta.finePrint: Every automation described on this page runs in production today.
